task_updates.executor_notes: Completed the remaining drag-side refactor around the existing `groupDragEntry` path: grouped drag state now flows through the heterogeneous move model, the old deferred-expansion symbols are gone, and overlap resolution consumes caller-provided extents while keeping `findBestGroupStart` as the shared t>=0 clamp.

Verified and preserved the interaction-guarded sync rewrite already present in `usePinnedShotGroups.ts`, and updated `reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows` to filter/restore via `children[]` while preserving `group.start`. The touched sync/commit regressions pass in the full relevant module run.

Verified the shot-drop path already builds pinned-group children from loop-local `timeOffset` state, pushes each child before incrementing the offset, and commits the resulting `buildGroupFromChildren(...)` output through the existing rows edit without consulting `workingData.config.clips`. The shot-drop regression test passes in the full touched-module run.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed `expandPinnedGroupDrag` and `deferredPinnedGroupClipIds` are fully removed, `planMultiDragMoves`/`applyMultiDragMoves` operate on clip/group move unions, and grouped placement still resolves through `findBestGroupStart`, which remains the t>=0 clamp.

Confirmed `usePinnedGroupSync` re-arms its debounce via `scheduleSync()` when `isInteractionActive()` is true, `pinGroup` delegates through `buildGroupFromConfigClips`, and sync updates pinned groups through `replaceGroupChildren` while preserving `group.start`.
Confirmed `useExternalDrop` builds `children` inline from the shot-drop loop state, pushes each child before `timeOffset += nextEdit.duration`, and never reads `workingData.config.clips`.
